blocks.body: Past Perfect Continuous: Time Expressions — for, since, how long, before, when, by the time, all day, all morning, until then, and other markers of duration before a past moment.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"Words like for, since, how long, before, when, by the time, all day, all morning, until then often point to Past Perfect Continuous because they show a process or duration before a past moment.","rules":[{"label":"for / since","color":"emerald","text":"These words show a duration or a starting point for a process before another past moment.","example":"They had been waiting for an hour. / She had been working since 7 a.m."},{"label":"how long","color":"blue","text":"This is a typical question about the duration of a process before a past event or moment.","example":"How long had you been studying before the exam?"},{"label":"before / when / by the time","color":"amber","text":"These expressions help connect the process to another past action or point in the past.","example":"He had been driving before the storm started. / By the time I arrived, she had been working all morning."},{"label":"all day / all morning / until then","color":"violet","text":"They highlight a longer process that continued up to that moment.","example":"They had been talking all morning. / We had been waiting until then."}]}

{"title":"1. Useful time expressions","intro":"Start with the most common markers of Past Perfect Continuous.","items":[{"label":"Duration","title":"for + period","subtitle":"for two hours / for a long time / for weeks."},{"label":"Starting point","title":"since + point in time","subtitle":"since 8 a.m. / since Monday / since breakfast."},{"label":"Past reference","title":"before / when / by the time","subtitle":"the process up to another event or moment in the past."},{"label":"Long process","title":"all day / all morning / until then","subtitle":"a longer background or duration up to that moment."}]}

{"title":"2. How these words work","sections":[{"label":"For and since","color":"emerald","description":"For gives a duration, and since gives the starting point of a process before a past moment.","examples":[{"en":"They had been waiting for thirty minutes before the bus came.","ua":"The duration came before the next event."},{"en":"She had been working since 7 a.m. when I arrived.","ua":"The starting point shows how far back the process began."}]},{"label":"How long","color":"blue","description":"Ask how long the process had lasted before another event in the past.","examples":[{"en":"How long had you been studying before the exam?","ua":"The question asks about duration."},{"en":"How long had they been living there before they moved?","ua":"The past event gives the limit."}]},{"label":"Before / when / by the time","color":"amber","description":"These expressions connect the process to another past action or point in the past.","examples":[{"en":"He had been running before the match started.","ua":"The process came earlier than the next event."},{"en":"By the time we arrived, she had been cooking all morning.","ua":"The process had continued up to that moment."}]},{"label":"All day / all morning / until then","color":"violet","description":"These expressions highlight a long action or background up to that point.","examples":[{"en":"They had been working all day before they went home.","ua":"The long process fills the earlier part of the day."},{"en":"We had been waiting until then.","ua":"The process continued up to that limit."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common combinations","intro":"See how time expressions work with Past Perfect Continuous.","rows":[{"en":"They had been waiting for an hour before the bus came.","ua":"duration before a past event","note":"for"},{"en":"She had been working since 7 a.m. when I arrived.","ua":"starting point before a past moment","note":"since"},{"en":"How long had you been studying before the exam?","ua":"a question about duration","note":"how long"},{"en":"By the time we called, he had been sleeping all morning.","ua":"a longer process before that moment","note":"by the time / all morning"}],"warning":"After had been, use V-ing, not V3. With when or by the time, the second event is often in Past Simple."}

{"title":"4. Short summary","items":["for gives duration, and since gives the starting point of a process before a past moment.","How long is a typical question about duration before a past event or point in the past.","before, when, and by the time connect the process to another past action.","all day, all morning, and until then highlight a longer background or process."]}
